$schema_note: COORD-140 [Memory] Phase 0 eval benchmark. Per coord/docs/MEMORY_ARCHITECTURE.md §8: this is the recall-with-citations harness EVERY later memory phase is measured against (recall@k, citation precision, answer groundedness). It encodes question -> expected-citation cases grounded in REAL repo history. It is DATA ONLY: it does NOT implement retrieval/recall (that is COORD-141). Every expected source below is a real, existing ticket / file / decision-record that coord/scripts/decision-extractor.test.js validates to exist. 'must_include' lists substrings a correct cited answer must contain; 'expected_sources' lists the hash-linked sources a correct answer must cite.

cases.question: Why was COORD-159 deferred and to which ticket?
cases.rationale: Decision-layer 'why' question answered straight from requirement_closure.deferred_to of the COORD-159 decision record.

cases.question: Which files define the conformance attestation signing (ed25519 over the journal chain head)?
cases.rationale: File-citation question. The ed25519 signing of the attestation subject lives in conformance-attestation.js; MEMORY_ARCHITECTURE.md §2 documents that the signed subject includes journal_chain_head.

cases.question: What broke when governed agents appended to the journal concurrently, and how was it repaired?
cases.rationale: Real multi-agent contention history: concurrent governed appends crossed prev_event_hash links and broke the hash-chain; COORD-124 added the guarded gov repair-chain capability to re-link and re-anchor it. Grounded in the COORD-124 decision record (critical_invariants + requirement_closure).

cases.question: Which ticket extends COORD-139's Memory architecture spec into the Phase 0 substrate, and what does it deliver?
cases.rationale: Decision-chain question across the [Memory] backlog: COORD-139 (the spec) defers the roadmap forward; COORD-140 is the Phase 0 substrate that emits decisions.ndjson + this benchmark. Grounded in the COORD-139 decision record's deferred_to and the architecture doc's phased plan.

cases.question: Why does baseline reproduction run in a throwaway git worktree instead of the live tree?
cases.rationale: Invariant-citation question. COORD-126 records the critical invariant that baseline computation is isolated in a throwaway git worktree and never mutates the live tree. Answered from the COORD-126 decision record's critical_invariants.
